cards.back: A virtualized datacenter is a computing environment where multiple physical servers are consolidated into a single virtual layer, providing scalability, flexibility, and resource sharing.
A hypervisor is a software layer that sits between a physical server and a virtual machine, allowing multiple virtual machines to run on a single physical server.

cards.front: What is a Cloud Platform Architecture?
cards.back: A cloud platform architecture refers to the design and organization of a cloud computing system, including the virtualized datacenter, hypervisors, and other components that enable scalability and flexibility.
A microservices architecture is a software design pattern that structures an application as a collection of small, independent services, each with its own responsibility and communication interface.

Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA) is a software design pattern that structures an application as a collection of services that communicate with each other to provide a specific business capability or functionality.
